candidates.direction_name: Restore Accepted Grouped-Rows-8 Hot-Band Consumer Ordering

score_breakdown.formula: rank_score + surface_diversity_bonus + queue_resolution_bonus + user_guidance_bonus + fresh_anchor_bonus - risk_penalty

ranking_notes: This ranks first because the exact PTX restore and the non-PTX sibling are now both confirmed plateau anchors rather than active search leaders.
A clean grouped_rows=8 recheck from the restored winner is the quickest way to decide whether this remaining alternate PTX surface still belongs in the live queue.
Even a negative result is valuable here because it shrinks the frontier with current evidence instead of leaving an outdated historical branch hanging.
candidates.hypothesis: The exact PTX restore and the non-PTX sibling have now both been remeasured on fresh rounds and both landed back in the same plateau regime. That means round 28 should stop spending budget on those two anchors alone and instead use the restored PTX winner to evaluate the last materially different PTX grouping surface that is still sitting in the frontier: grouped_rows=8 consumer ordering. This branch already has one older loss, but it changes CTA grouping and consumer locality more materially than another frozen micro-retime. Running it once from the clean anchor is the fastest way to decide whether it deserves to stay in the live queue at all.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row traversal and consumer-order locality in the PTX hot-band path, not the already-replayed exact PTX restore and not another same-plateau alternate-surface A/B.
candidates.risk: Medium. This family already has one measured loss, so the main risk is spending a round to confirm that the alternate grouping surface is still inferior. That confirmation is still useful because it would let the loop close this branch with current evidence instead of leaving it half-open in the frontier.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the grouped_rows=8 surface remains materially behind the exact PTX winner and the non-PTX sibling without a clearly better scheduler signature.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
ranking_notes: This stays second as the explicit safety valve after any alternate-surface miss.
It is intentionally not first because another same-surface restore would now be pure noise chasing.
If dir_01 loses cleanly, the loop can recover to the winner immediately.
candidates.hypothesis: The exact PTX winner remains the safety anchor. It just remeasured at 24.167423 ms, which is close enough to the recorded best that further immediate restore-only rounds would be noise chasing. It stays second only as the low-risk fallback if the grouped_rows=8 surface immediately misses.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: No new bottleneck is being attacked here; this is the exact recovery path back to the strongest measured anchor.
candidates.risk: Low. This is the exact best measured surface.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop once the exact PTX winner surface is restored or if it no longer reproduces the prior plateau anchor.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Grouped-Row Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling Surface
ranking_notes: This remains in the set as the strongest exact alternate surface after the PTX winner.
It drops to third because round 26 already proved what it can prove: it is stable and close, but not superior.
Keep it available in case both dir_01 and dir_02 need a correctness-proven non-PTX fallback.
candidates.hypothesis: The non-PTX sibling is now a known-good plateau alternate. It remains useful as the third-ranked branch because it is still the cleanest exact-surface A/B against the PTX winner, but after round 26 it no longer deserves the main search budget unless the grouped_rows=8 recheck also misses.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: PTX-microkernel-specific control and export coupling on the current winner surface, while preserving the same broad 128x128 footprint and grouped-row locality.
candidates.risk: Low to medium. This is an exact restore of a measured-correct alternate surface, but it is now clearly bounded by the same plateau that the winner reproduces.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the sibling surface continues to reproduce the same plateau signature without any separation from the PTX winner.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
